raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Section makes a core contribution claim—learned contrastive selector vs map-averaging—whose thesis clarity and gap framing are central to any review.",
    "R1.2": "Compressed pipeline description chains multiple logical steps (NMS at IoU 0.3, contrastive training, partial-AUC calibration) with tense drift; logical coherence between premises and stated conclusions must be checked.",
    "R1.3": "Empirical claims (0.91 sensitivity at 1 FP/scan, robustness under slice-thickness variation) must be traced to the 412-scan evaluation and assessed for whether hedging is sufficient given the evidence base.",
    "R1.4": "Section explicitly positions against Setio-style FROC ensembles; reviewing requires verifying the novelty delta is substantiated rather than merely asserted.",
    "R1.6": "Multiple run-on sentences, non-parallel list structure, and pervasive past-perfect drift ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated') signal that prose polish is a live concern regardless of review scope."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review cover scientific/technical substance (methodology soundness, evidence-claim alignment, novelty positioning) in addition to prose quality, or is the scope limited to writing and clarity only?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Section makes a core contribution claim—learned contrastive selector vs map-averaging—whose thesis clarity and gap framing are central to any review.
rationale.R1.2: Compressed pipeline description chains multiple logical steps (NMS at IoU 0.3, contrastive training, partial-AUC calibration) with tense drift; logical coherence between premises and stated conclusions must be checked.
rationale.R1.3: Empirical claims (0.91 sensitivity at 1 FP/scan, robustness under slice-thickness variation) must be traced to the 412-scan evaluation and assessed for whether hedging is sufficient given the evidence base.
rationale.R1.4: Section explicitly positions against Setio-style FROC ensembles; reviewing requires verifying the novelty delta is substantiated rather than merely asserted.
rationale.R1.6: Multiple run-on sentences, non-parallel list structure, and pervasive past-perfect drift ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated') signal that prose polish is a live concern regardless of review scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review cover scientific/technical substance (methodology soundness, evidence-claim alignment, novelty positioning) in addition to prose quality, or is the scope limited to writing and clarity only?
